title: Update agent skill to v2 socket protocol
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

## Problem

`skill/crossbridge/SKILL.md` documents the v1 polling-bridge workflow, not the
shipped v2 socket-based architecture.

Concrete drift:

- Tells agents to label issues with `xb-target:<slug>` / `xb-status:open` and
  wait for "the bridge daemon" to copy them. That label-driven flow is only
  consumed by the legacy polling binary in `crossbridge/`. The v2 server does
  not poll labels — clients must call `crossbridge-client submit/answer`.
- Recommends `crossbridge-request` / `crossbridge-answer` shell helpers as the
  primary entry points. Those scripts (in `script/`) only write the v1 labels;
  under v2 they are inert.
- "Manual fallback" sections write the same v1 labels — also inert under v2.
- Footer says "the bridge runs every ~30s", which is v1 polling-cycle language.
  v2 is event-driven via Unix sockets.
- Never mentions `crossbridge-client` despite that being the only v2 entry
  point an agent can use today.

Net effect: an agent following this skill will appear to send a request but
nothing on the v2 transport will fire.

## What v2 actually wants the agent to do

- ask: `crossbridge-client submit --issue <id> --target <slug>` (after
  creating the issue with `crosslink issue create`).
- answer: `crossbridge-client answer --issue <id>` once a `result` comment
  has been added.
- check: list inbound/outbound issues by `xb:inbound` / `xb:outbound` label.
- The server appends a footer to every inbound issue's body telling the agent
  to run `crossbridge-client answer --issue <id>` (see
  `ANSWER_INSTRUCTION_FOOTER` in `crossbridge-server/src/handler.rs:23`).

## Acceptance

- `skill/crossbridge/SKILL.md` only references the v2 client commands.
- All `xb-target:` / `xb-status:open` instructions removed.
- "Manual fallback" sections either deleted or rewritten to invoke
  `crossbridge-client` directly.
- Polling-language ("every ~30s") replaced with the event-driven reality.
- Cross-check against `.design/client.md`, `.design/server.md`, and
  `crossbridge-server/src/handler.rs` so the skill matches real behavior.

## Related

- See companion issue for removal of the v1 `crossbridge/` crate; once removed,
  `script/crossbridge-request` / `script/crossbridge-answer` and any remaining
  references should be deleted as part of that work.
